meta-description: Learn about high deductible health plans by reviewing the definition in the HealthCare.gov Glossary.
title: High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP)
excerpt: A plan with a higher deductible than a traditional insurance plan. The monthly premium is usually lower, but you pay more health care costs yourself before the insurance company starts to pay its share (your deductible). A high deductible plan (HDHP) can be combined with a health savings account (HSA), allowing you to pay for certain medical expenses with money free from federal taxes.
content:
A plan with a higher deductible than a traditional insurance plan. The monthly premium is usually lower, but you pay more health care costs yourself before the insurance company starts to pay its share (also called your deductible).

A high deductible plan can be combined with a health savings account (HSA), for you to pay for certain medical expenses with money you set aside in your tax-free HSA. This is why it’s more commonly called an HSA-eligible plan.

Related Content

  • Learn about HSA-eligible plans, including the minimum and maximum yearly deductible amounts.
  • Read IRS.gov Publication 969 for details on HSAs, and discover if you qualify.
